name: Tina Smith
subject: Did you see Trump's budget proposal?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
The best way for us to fight back is to make our voices heard.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-march?refcode=rtw_20190314_em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Sturgeon Lake&state=MN&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto") Friend, Donald Trump just released his budget and his priorities are clear: More for the wealthy and less for working people. Trump's budget cuts Medicare by nearly $1 trillion and slashes Medicaid, Social Security, and student loan assistance. If we don't fight back, Republicans would decimate essential programs millions of middle-class and low-income Americans rely on. **[The best way for you to take action in this moment is to make our voices heard -- and that's why I'm asking you to sign on before midnight in opposition to Trump's proposal. Can I count on you to add your name as one of 5,000 folks we need from Minnesota by midnight?](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d849/c1f7015/612356e2/1140595e/1211899249/VEsC/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201903_budget-ld?source=rtw_20190314_em-ld")** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d849/c1f7015/612356e2/1140595e/1211899249/VEsD/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201903_budget-ld?source=rtw_20190314_em-ld") Our budget should be a reflection of our values as a country. We should be prioritizing education, affordable housing, infrastructure, and environmental protections. Instead, Trump is cutting programs that working Americans rely on so that he can fund his border wall and the GOP's tax cuts for the wealthy. **[I won't stand for this -- and I need you fighting with me, friend. Will you add your name right now to stand against Trump's budget? break through the noise in this crowded primary.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Original message from Kamala Harris, March 14, 2019

We are facing something unprecedented: a record number of Democrats --
including a record number of women and people of color -- are running for
President. This is greatly encouraging for the future of our country, but
there is still a long path ahead of us.

I look forward to engaging in substantive debates with each of these
candidates -- including the newest to join the race today, Beto O’Rourke
-- and ultimately selecting a Democratic nominee who will take on and beat
Donald Trump in November of 2020.

This will not be an ordinary election, and we have a lot of work to do.
Running a competitive, winning campaign will require building and
sustaining a robust grassroots organizing program to get our message to
voters across the country.

That’s why I’m asking: If you are with me in this campaign, can you pitch
in a donation
today? We are just a few weeks out from the first FEC fundraising deadline
of our campaign, and we need to continue building on our momentum in these
early months:

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-190314-mm-10?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190314-2-full&amount=10&express_lane=true&t=1007&refcode2=3390_5755789_bt_nf_&akid=3390%2E5755789%2Ebt_nf_ ]Chip in $10 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-190314-mm-10?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190314-2-full&amount=100&express_lane=true&t=1010&refcode2=3390_5755789_bt_nf_&akid=3390%2E5755789%2Ebt_nf_ ]Chip in $100 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-190314-mm-10?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190314-2-full&amount=250&express_lane=true&t=1011&refcode2=3390_5755789_bt_nf_&akid=3390%2E5755789%2Ebt_nf_ ]Chip in $250 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-190314-mm-10?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190314-2-full&t=1012&refcode2=3390_5755789_bt_nf_&akid=3390%2E5755789%2Ebt_nf_ ]Another Amount

I’m running for president because I love my country. And I’m running to be
president of the people, by the people, and for all people.

To run a winning campaign, we are going to have to cut through the noise
of this crowded primary and share our message with as many voters as
possible. That’s why I am planning to spend as much time as possible out
on the campaign trail, meeting with communities across the country,
listening to the issues affecting them.

I know that, with your support, we will share our stories, organize across
the country, and we can win this fight and the many fights ahead --
together.

For The People,

— Kamala Harris

body:
This is pretty important
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=738%2E150750%2Ez_7xPe)

Sisters and Brothers -
The primary field is getting larger. That means the fight ahead will only get harder.
But what we are talking about is not just winning the primary and beating Trump — this campaign is about building a movement capable of taking on the drug companies, the insurance companies, the military and prison industrial complex, the fossil fuel industry and the rest of the billionaire class.
That’s what Bernie’s entire life has been about. He did not come to these ideas lately.
And today is a great day to make a donation to say that’s what you’re about, as well. So we have to ask:
[**Can you make a $3 donation to our campaign today? We’re shooting for as many individual donations as we can possibly generate. This matters.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/9861?refcode=em190314-3-120&t=26&akid=738%2E150750%2Ez_7xPe)
We have an important FEC deadline around the corner and it’s not just about how much money we’ve raised, but how many donations are powering our political revolution. Because that is where our power to create change comes from.
Not just Bernie. Us.
In solidarity,
Team Bernie

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Take a look if you can
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
I want to send you another note before the day is up. It’s an important one. It’s not lost on us that there are more than a few candidates running for the Democratic nomination. We’ll start behind them in fundraising and staffing.

#
We launched our campaign for president this morning and I’ve been encouraged by the extraordinary support from people all over the country. I want to send you another note before the day is up. It’s an important one.
It’s not lost on us that there are more than a few candidates running for the Democratic nomination. We’ll start behind them in fundraising and staffing. Some of these candidates will have big super PACs and special interests backing them. We won’t.
**Our campaign will be different.** We won’t be taking a dime from lobbyists, PACs, or corporations. We will fund this effort without help from any special interests so that we can be sure our democracy is once again powered by people, and only people.
What we raise in the first 24 hours will set the tone in the national conversation about the viability of our campaign. **If everyone reading this right now gives just a little bit, we will be in good shape.**
[Can we count on you to donate $5? Today is the best time to make a contribution so we can show everyone how strong this campaign is out of the gate.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16909?refcode=ema20190314-4&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=25000%2E444211%2ENWy2SD)
The challenges we face are the greatest in living memory. The only path forward is a campaign by all of us, for all of us. Let’s give this our best shot.
Beto
**[Contribute »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16909?refcode=ema20190314-4&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=25000%2E444211%2ENWy2SD)**

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=25000%2E444211%2ENWy2SD). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

name: My Faith Votes
subject: Lifting up the Poor
body:
| | Star Parker explains a solution that is proven to work
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRW3we3u8oKwtxLPg1pS-XOBuaI4vqMGV7Z78Jx19HcLJbPWUE7lhcwSIYtLblhs9_Pp3NWY4bXqJoyKfBAqmoFwaFEkSsuPFkCQB1anxssP&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190315com01&n=1)
| Peter, One of the challenging topics politicians and voters frequently discuss is the best way to lift others out of poverty. Our friend and My Faith Votes influencer, Star Parker, tackles this important topic and provides a synopsis of how our nation is doing today on this critical issue. _“In politics, we hear comparisons between the haves and the have-nots. The wealthy and the poor. The one percent and the lower class. We’ve listened to the calls for wealth redistribution, free college and Medicare for all. And this rhetoric will only continue to increase with 2020 right around the corner. Yet the proven way to fight poverty is not welfare; it's encouraging personal responsibility and driving market-based solutions.”_
–Star Parker Star’s insights will provide you with new information and a fresh perspective on poverty. We encourage you to [read the article](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRW3we3u8oKwtxLPg1pS-XOBuaI4vqMGV7Z78Jx19HcLJbPWUE7lhcwSIYtLblhs9_Pp3NWY4bXqJoyKfBAqmoFwaFEkSsuPFkCQB1anxssP&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190315com01&n=2) and share it with your friends. # Rising Wages = Workers Winning

“The recent jump in paychecks has come with an unusual characteristic, as workers at the lower end of the pay scale are getting the greater benefit,” Jeff Cox reports for CNBC.

“Average hourly earnings rose 3.4 percent in February from the same period a year ago, according to a Bureau of Labor Statistics report last week. That’s the biggest gain since April 2009 and seventh month in a row that compensation has been 3 percent or better.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d6b6b41648&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump launched another battle for border security on Monday, calling for $8.6 billion in funding for the Wall in his proposed federal budget for next year. Predictably, top Democrats came out swinging, bashing a border wall as ‘expensive and ineffective,’” Betsy McCaughey writes in the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8b177f6658&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Truth is, the Democrats aren’t leveling with the public about the billions we are already forced to spend on shelters, food, diapers, medical care and child care for migrants sneaking across the border and claiming asylum.”
---
|
---
| “Japanese automaker Toyota is upping its investment in the U.S. by 30 percent to $13 billion by 2021, the company said Thursday. The company pledged in 2017 to invest $10 billion in its U.S. factories over five years,” Robert Ferris reports for [CNBC](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1d155fb64f&e=3ee4985eea). “The new plan includes adding 600 jobs at U.S. manufacturing plants.”
---
|
---
| “First Lady Melania and President Donald Trump have participated in a traditional ceremony with the Irish Prime Minister to mark St. Patrick's Day, receiving a bowl of shamrock from the Irish leader. Taoiseach Leo Varadkar, as the Irish premier is properly titled, attended the ceremony in the East Room of the White House on Thursday evening,” Keith Griffith reports in the _[Daily Mail](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=147455072d&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| President Trump’s “‘Budget for a Better America’ proposes to strengthen work requirements in programs such as food stamps and Temporary Assistance for Needy Families, or TANF, to help work-capable adults move toward greater self-support,” Vijay Menon writes in _[The Daily Signal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=391f3aebc1&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Nearly 90 percent of Americans say they agree with the principle that adults who are able to work should be required to work or prepare for work in exchange for government assistance.”
---
|

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: This is so important >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2a3/2a380d31/3a082694/f1035f/3533340350/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2a3/2a380d31/3a082694/f1035f/3533340350/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hi there,
My friend Senator Maggie Hassan knows better than anyone else how competitive New Hampshire elections can be, so she said that I could reach out.
I'm about to ask you to make a critical donation to my Senate reelection campaign. But first, I want to explain why this campaign is so important.
Everywhere I go, I hear from people who are fired up about making Trump a one-term president -- and I couldn't agree more. But this isn't just about Trump; it's about fighting for solutions on health care, on climate change and on so many of our other priorities that have been ignored by his administration for the past two years.
**And the truth is, no matter who our next president is, we won 't be able to do any of that unless we take back control of the Senate.**
As long as Republicans control the Senate, a Democratic president would be blocked at every turn. That's why right-wing groups spent hundreds of millions of dollars to protect their majority in 2018, and that's why they're planning to spend even more in 2020.
The GOP is desperate to protect his Senate majority -- and the disastrous agenda - and this year, that means going after my seat. They have already named me one of their top targets, and right-wing groups are already running ads against me. **Now, after a poll just showed me tied with GOP Gov. Chris Sununu, the attacks will only get worse.**
**Look, Democrats cannot take back the Senate unless we protect New Hampshire 's Senate seat -- but we can't defeat the Trump agenda unless grassroots donors like you give now in the early stages of this campaign:**
[**Please, rush $3 or more now so that we can defend New Hampshire 's Senate seat and take back the Senate for Democrats!**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2a3/2a380d31/3a082694/f10358/3533340350/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0Y2pzIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxOTAzMTVfTVdINENKUzFfNm1vIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4cjs?refcode=e20190315_MWH4CJS1_6mo&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
With your help, we can do more than just defeat Trump and Senate Republicans in 2020. We can defeat their whole harmful, hateful, right-wing agenda once and for all.
Thank you for your support today. I'm so grateful to have you on this team,
Jeanne

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Fwd: update
body:
Hey I wanted to loop you
in on something.

Angelique, our Finance Director, emailed me last night to say that our
fundraising was taking a small dip and that we may not be able to make it
up down the road.

The way things are currently looking, we are just short of our projections
leading up to the first FEC deadline of our campaign. I don’t want to
begin this journey with you by falling short of our first goal.

So I’m asking: will you add a
contribution before the first FEC deadline of the campaign? We’re not
taking a dime from corporate PACs or federal lobbyists, just people. So
your contribution is crucial to reaching this goal:

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

Thank you for everything you do.

— Kamala Harris

---------- Forwarded message ----------

From: Angelique Cannon
Date: Thursday, Mar 14, 2019 at 10:23 PM
Subject: update
To: Kamala Harris

Sorry Kamala, I wish I had better news about today's fundraising numbers
-- but unfortunately, we’re not on pace right now to reach our goal before
the first FEC deadline.

The last thing I want to do is worry you about this. I know you have
enough on your plate already with everything that’s going on in Washington
and on the campaign trail. But I’m sitting here racking my brain and doing
everything I can think of.

If you have a moment to send an email to the best people on our grassroots
team asking them to chip in, it would be a huge help. Even just $5, $10,
or $25 from a lot of people would do the trick.

Thanks so much for your help on this.

ac

Angelique Cannon
Finance Director
Kamala 2020

body:
It's Lincoln Day Dinner season in Indiana! So we're hitting the road!Our team at your Indiana Republican Party has a busy schedule ahead of us a...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/hitting-the-road?e=1acd9bb3d6)

Hitting the Road

It's Lincoln Day Dinner season in Indiana! So we're hitting the road!

Our team at your Indiana Republican Party has a busy schedule ahead of us attending these great local events that are organized by local party leaders at the county and township level. Tonight, I'm excited to be attending the Hamilton County Lincoln Day Dinner, where I'll be interviewing Governor Eric Holcomb as we discuss how he's continuing to deliver results for Hoosiers (check out the livesteam here (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) at about 7:30 p.m. ET).

>From there, I'll be attending my home county's event in Madison County tomorrow night, before keynoting the Lincoln Day Dinners in Montgomery County on Monday and in Hancock County next Friday.

Hosting these events, which serve as primary fundraisers for your local Republican Party, is a lot of work, so the next time you see your local party chair (and his or her team), a big high five is definitely in order.

Check out the (growing) list of Lincoln Day Dinners below! Hope you see you there!

-Chairman Kyle Hupfer

PHOTO: Chairman Hupfer delivers a speech discussing our party's 2018 victories and our 2019 goals to close out the 2019 Indiana Republican Party Spring Dinner.
GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS

Taking Indiana to the World!

This week, Governor Eric Holcomb has yet again taken Indiana to the world! As part of his fourth economic development mission to Europe, he’s been meeting with business and government leaders across Belgium, France and Germany, all with one goal – to bring more jobs, investment and opportunity home to Indiana! And as a veteran of the U.S. Navy, Governor Holcomb also paid tribute to those brave men and women who serve our nation...today and yesterday. His trip included:
* Meeting with the leaders of European-based businesses interested in locating operations (and more jobs!) in Indiana
* Hosting roundtables with global businesses that already employ Hoosiers.
* Touring a subsidiary of Indiana-based Hillenbrand in Germany to discuss operations and their global relationships.
* Visiting with and thanking Hoosier servicemen and women at Ramstein Air Base in Germany – HQ for the U.S. Air Force in Europe.
* Touring the Normandy American Cemetery in France to pay respect to the Hoosiers, and all Allied service members, who gave their lives during the Battle of Normandy.
* Meeting with the U.S. Ambassadors to Germany, Belgium, France and NATO to discuss opportunities for Indiana’s relationships to grow with businesses in these countries!

Today, European businesses have more than 450 facilities in Indiana – which employ more than 113,200 Hoosiers. And with trips like this, that number’s only going to keep going up! Learn more about Indiana's economic ties with Europe here (https://gallery.mailchimp.com/1c7397d5fea5d818a66609a67/files/5fa9e3d3-554f-4265-a05c-15e1e8b5ad07/Europe_Factsheet_2019.pdf?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .
http://beatthegovernor.mayhem.cbssports.com/brackets/pre-selection-sunday?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Beat Governor Holcomb (with Your Bracket)!

It’s March in Indiana, so you know what that means. Get your brackets ready, because it's time to Beat the Governor with a spirited bracket challenge (http://beatthegovernor.mayhem.cbssports.com/brackets/?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) as part of the 2019 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament! Your selection window opens on Sunday night, and closes on Thursday. Top-placing brackets in this year’s Beat the Governor bracket challenge will win must-have prizes, including:

First & Second Place
A basketball signed by Governor Eric Holcomb

Third Place
Any item of your choice from the Indiana GOP store (https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f7edae2df1&e=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

See how your bracket matches up with picks from Governor Holcomb, Chairman Hupfer, and your fellow Republicans statewide! Save this link (http://beatthegovernor.mayhem.cbssports.com/brackets/?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and make sure to add your bracket to the Beat the Governor bracket challenge!

Get to Know Franklin Mayor Steve Barnett!

It’s a municipal election year in Indiana! Across the state, we’ve got Republican elected officials who are working hard to deliver results for their communities, and one of them is Franklin Mayor Steve Barnett (https://www.facebook.com/MayorBarnett/?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) . He became mayor of Franklin, a city just south of Indianapolis, in 2017, and he’s using his experience in management, eight years on the city council and five years on the board of public works to help make Franklin a great place to live, work and play.

Learn more about Franklin Mayor Steve Barnett:

What are your biggest priorities for Franklin?
Most important is providing a clean environment. I’m also focused on public safety, quality of life, infrastructure improvements and economic development.

What’s your vision for Franklin in 10 years?
We’re working hard to see completion of the I-65 exit development, as well as completion of the downtown amphitheater. We’ll also have the trails complete of U.S. 31 and an expanded business park development.

What’s your favorite memory during your time in office so far?
I’ve really enjoyed working with the mayor’s youth council to build Unity Park, an inclusive playground for all children. You can read more about that playground and our effort here (http://www.dailyjournal.net/2018/06/11/new_inclusive_park_opens_in_franklin/?mc_cid=6d7253158b&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

How do you plan to get residents more involved in the decision-making process for your city?
I have an open door policy at the Mayor's Office. I would also like to hold quarterly town hall meetings.

Are you a Republican mayoral candidate (without a Republican primary challenger) who would like to be featured in an upcoming edition of The Party Line? Contact us at info@indiana.gop (mailto:info@indiana.gop) , and we’ll be in touch soon!

Lincoln Day Dinner Season is in Full Gear!

Wow! In the last week, your local Republican Party teams have organized a range of successful Lincoln Day Dinners – including a screening of Glory and a speech from Congressman Jim Baird in the western part of the state (Warren, Fountain and Parke counties), speeches from Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch and former Congressman Todd Rokita in the north central part of the state (Tipton and Clinton counties) and speeches from Senator Todd Young, Secretary of State Connie Lawson and State Sen. (and Indianapolis mayoral candidate) Jim Merritt in three Marion County townships (Pike, Perry and Warren). And tonight, we have big events in Hamilton and Wells counties!

Next week, we have even more action! Check below for details about next week’s Lincoln Day Dinners in Madison, Montgomery, Vanderburgh, Rush and Hancock counties!
